title: Finland: Fortum and City of Kemi to Develop Area for Hydrogen Production on Ajos Island
content: Fortum and the City of Kemi have agreed to develop a site located on Kemiâ€™s Ajos Island for a hydrogen and/or hydrogen derivatives production plant. The area is approximately 17 hectares and is located on the western part of the island. Under the agreement, Fortum will develop and market the site to clean transition industrial players, while the actual investment in the plant will be made by the project investor. Kimmo Heikka, Vitality Director for the City of Kemi, noted that if realised, the project will create new jobs, increase tax revenues, and strengthen long-term vitality in the region. Jyrki Holappa, Customer Site Development Director at Fortum, highlighted that the Ajos area offers excellent conditions for a hydrogen project due to the proximity to the Port of Kemi and the availability of biogenic carbon dioxide. With this project, Fortum aims to drive the energy transition based on low-carbon electricity production.
author: Analysts Account
